items.label: Setup S-1
items.nl: Finite-alphabet i.i.d. observational world, in TWO explicitly bridged views. (a) CANONICAL / statement view: one observed unit is O = (X, A, Y) : Fin d x Bool x Bool; the single-observation law P is a probability Measure (built from a PMF) on that finite type with ARBITRARY cell masses; the n-sample law is the finite i.i.d. product Measure.pi (fun _ : Fin n => P), and every statement, estimator (a measurable Fin n -> Obs -> R) and the minimax inf/sup live HERE. (b) AMBIENT view, used only to import IIDSample-typed Causalean declarations: the infinite i.i.d. product Omega_inf = (ℕ -> Obs) with mu_inf = MeasureTheory.Measure.infinitePi (fun _ : ℕ => P), whose coordinate process Z i omega = omega i is a genuine Causalean.Stat.IIDSample (ℕ -> Obs) Obs mu_inf P. BRIDGE (both directions available, in scope, for arbitrary P): Causalean.Stat.iidSample_finN_pushforward pushes mu_inf forward along the first-n-coordinates map onto Measure.pi (fun _ : Fin n => P), so any event/integral of the first n coordinates has equal mass in the two views. Primitives p_k = P(X=k), pi_k = P(A=1|X=k), mu_{ak} = E[Y|A=a,X=k], q_{aky} = P(X=k,A=a,Y=y) and the four-cell vector q_k are DERIVED real-valued readouts of the single-observation law P (finite sums over atoms), not independent fields. Scalars epsilon in (0,1/2], c in (0,inf) are plain reals. — HIGHEST-LEVERAGE REUSE DECISION, and the closest analogue in the library. Causalean.Estimation.MinimaxATE.Model realizes ALMOST exactly this world: Obs C = C x Bool x Bool over a Fintype C, binary treatment and binary outcome, obsReal/obsPMF/obsLaw the single-observation law, productLaw = Measure.pi over Fin n, nMSE the n-sample MSE, minimaxMiss the sup-over-class miss, plus the Chebyshev/Markov bridge nMiss_sq_le_nMSE. BLOCKING MISMATCH (verified by reading Causalean/Estimation/MinimaxATE/Model.lean lines 87-89 and 137-138): obsReal HARDCODES a UNIFORM covariate marginal (Fintype.card C)^{-1}, and `ate g` averages with the same uniform weights. This note's entire d^2/(n^2 log^2 n) rate is driven by ARBITRARY, in particular rare, cell masses p_k, so the uniform-P_X model is a strictly different (and for this result degenerate) world -> NOT a verbatim reuse; define-local a p_k-parameterized law in Basic.lean, keeping the Obs/productLaw/nMSE naming and construction pattern one-to-one so F3 can port MinimaxATE proof idioms. *** IIDSample BRIDGE (round-2 correction FLAG-IIDSample-world; see decisions.iid_world_is_finite_product_with_infinite_ambient_view). *** Causalean.Stat.IIDSample is the sampling primitive the concentration engines are typed over (Concentration.hoeffding_ge : (S : IIDSample Omega X mu P) -> ... -> mu.real {omega | eps <= S.sampleMean f n omega - integral f} <= exp(...), verified at Stat/Concentration/TailBounds/Hoeffding.lean:87-91), but it is a structure over an AMBIENT mu carrying an ℕ-INDEXED process Z : ℕ -> Omega -> X with iIndepFun Z mu (Stat/Sample.lean:42-48). The finite coordinate process of Measure.pi (fun _ : Fin n => P) is Fin n-indexed and therefore CANNOT itself be an IIDSample -- the prior plan's claim that this world 'exposes an IIDSample view of the product law' was wrong, and PiTransport does not repair it (iidSample_finN_pushforward CONSUMES an IIDSample to produce the finite product, it does not produce one). The world therefore carries view (b) EXPLICITLY: Basic.lean defines `iidOfInfinitePi (P) : IIDSample (ℕ -> Obs) Obs (Measure.infinitePi (fun _ : ℕ => P)) P` with Z := fun i omega => omega i, meas := measurable_pi_apply, indep := ProbabilityTheory.iIndepFun_infinitePi (fun _ => measurable_id), identDist/law := MeasureTheory.Measure.infinitePi_map_eval -- COMPILED 0-sorry against this repo's pinned Mathlib, so this is a verified ~10-line def, not a conjecture -- together with the transport corollary `finProductLaw_eq_map` : (Measure.infinitePi (fun _ : ℕ => P)).map (fun omega (k : Fin n) => omega k) = Measure.pi (fun _ : Fin n => P), which is Causalean.Stat.iidSample_finN_pushforward APPLIED to iidOfInfinitePi (its Z k omega = omega k reduces definitionally). F3's usage pattern for every IIDSample-typed engine: state the target event as a measurable set E in Set (Fin n -> Obs), apply the engine on Omega_inf, then move the bound across with Causalean.Stat.event_pullback_along_iidSample / Measure.map_apply. This holds for ARBITRARY P (infinitePi needs only IsProbabilityMeasure P, which the PMF construction supplies), so it adds NO hypothesis to any statement and imposes no restriction on the cell masses -- exactly the point of keeping the finite product as the canonical statement world. Search trace for the bridge: loogle 'MeasureTheory.Measure.infinitePi' and 'ProbabilityTheory.iIndepFun _ (MeasureTheory.Measure.infinitePi _)' -> Measure.infinitePi / infinitePi_map_eval / measurePreserving_eval_infinitePi (Mathlib.Probability.ProductMeasure) and iIndepFun_infinitePi (Mathlib.Probability.Independence.InfinitePi), all four #check-confirmed present in the pinned toolchain; globbed Causalean/**/Sample/**/*.lean -> PiTransport.lean is the ONLY Sample bridge file and carries only the IIDSample -> Measure.pi direction (read in full, 93 lines) -> the finite-to-infinite view is absent from Causalean and is built here. Search trace: read ../doc/API.md sections '## 13sa. Estimation/MinimaxATE/' and '## 12. Stat/' + '### 12a. Stat/Sample.lean'; grepped Causalean/Estimation/MinimaxATE/Model.lean for structure/def/theorem; grepped Causalean for 'alphabet' -> no large-alphabet / arbitrary-discrete-mass observational model found.

items.label: Setup S-2
items.nl: Potential-outcome overlay on S1: binary potential outcomes Y(0), Y(1) : Omega -> Bool on the same finite probability space as (X, A, Y), with consistency (Y = Y(A) a.s.) and conditional exchangeability ((Y(0),Y(1)) indep A | X). Used only by the witness cluster; the estimation/minimax cluster is stated on the observed-data law alone. — Causalean.PO (POSystem, PO.Assumptions.Consistency, PO.Assumptions.IndepCF) is the framework analogue but is regime/graph-indexed over general measurable outcome spaces -- far heavier than the two-point binary overlay needed here, where def:two-category-witness supplies its OWN explicit full-data coupling ((Y(0),Y(1)) = (0, 1{X=1}), then A | X=k ~ Bernoulli(pi_k)) and prop:two-category-confounding VERIFIES consistency/exchangeability on it directly. Define-local over Mathlib with naming aligned to Causalean.PO. Search trace: read ../doc/API.md '## 8h. PO/' index (PO/Core/Regime, PO/Core/System, PO/Assumptions/Consistency, PO/Assumptions/IndepCF, PO/ID/Exact/ATE); no finite-alphabet binary PO overlay to reuse verbatim -> not found.

items.label: Setup S-3
items.nl: Functional / experiment-class / risk layer over S1: the overlap-restricted four-cell cone C_eps as a Set of four-vectors, the homogeneous cell contribution phi, the ATE functional tau(P) = sum_k phi(q_k), the experiment class E_{n,d,eps} of overlap-satisfying product laws, and the minimax risk R_{n,d,eps} = inf over measurable estimators of the sup-over-class MSE. — The risk shape (inf over measurable estimators of sup over a class of the n-sample MSE) is exactly Causalean.Estimation.MinimaxATE.Model's minimaxMiss/nMSE pattern, but over the p_k-parameterized class of S1 rather than the uniform-P_X structure-agnostic class, and in MSE (not miss-probability) form -> define-local, importing nMiss_sq_le_nMSE's Markov-bridge idiom. Search trace: grepped ../doc/API.md for 'Minimax' -> Stat/Minimax/{TotalVariation,LeCam,Pinsker,Scheffe,ChiSquared,ChiSquaredFinite,MinimaxRisk,Fano,Assouad,Mixture} and Estimation/MinimaxATE; Stat/Minimax/MinimaxRisk.lean supplies the two-point REDUCTIONS (reused by lem:one-category-bernoulli-lower) but carries no packaged inf-sup minimax-risk DEFINITION over a law class -> the risk def is not found and is build-inline.

items.label: Setup S-4
items.nl: Estimator layer over S1: the balanced deterministic split I_0 = {1..floor(n/2)}, I_1 = the rest; split cell counts N^{(j)}_{aky}; four-cell exponent multi-indices r in N_0^4 with |r| = sum r_{ay}; the falling factorial z_r; the pilot-certified heavy/light partition (Hhat_n, Lhat_n); the light-cell factorial-moment polynomial contribution That_L; and the truncated hybrid tauhat_n^hyb. — z_r is Mathlib's Nat.descFactorial (z_r = z.descFactorial r, with z_0 = 1 definitional) -- REUSED, not redefined; Causalean.Stat.injectiveTupleCount_eq_descFactorial (Stat/UStatistic/OrderM/Basic.lean) is the exact combinatorial bridge 'ordered injective tuple count = falling factorial' that the note's eq (9) unbiasedness E[U_{k,r}] = q_k^r rests on, and is reused as the counting engine in Helpers/FactorialMoments.lean. Causalean.Stat.SampleSplit / OneShotSplit is the analogue for the two-fold split but is a RANDOM cross-fitting split over a growing index family; the note fixes a DETERMINISTIC balanced split I_0/I_1 of Fin n, so the split itself is a two-line Finset definition -> build-inline. Search trace: grepped ../doc/API.md for 'descFactorial|fallingFactorial' -> Stat/UStatistic/OrderM/Basic.lean only; grepped ../doc/API.md and Causalean/ for 'factorial moment' -> not found (no factorial-moment unbiased-lift estimator layer exists); grepped Causalean for 'Chebyshev' (case-insensitive) -> all 15 hits are Chebyshev's INEQUALITY (Experimentation/DesignBased/Chebyshev.lean, Stat/Concentration, Mathlib/InformationTheory/Fano), none is the Chebyshev POLYNOMIAL; Mathlib's Polynomial.Chebyshev.T (Mathlib.RingTheory.Polynomial.Chebyshev, confirmed via loogle with T_one/T_mul/T_natAbs) is the reused primitive for T_M.

items.label: Definition P-1
items.nl: \(\mathcal E_{n,d,\epsilon}:=\{P^{\otimes n}: P\in\mathsf{Prob}(\{1,\ldots,d\}\times\{0,1\}^2),\ n,d\in\mathbb N,\ 0<\epsilon\le1/2,\text{ and }P\text{ satisfies }\mathrm{ass:overlap}\}\).

items.label: Definition P-5
items.nl:
Set \(I_0=\{1,\ldots,\lfloor n/2\rfloor\}\), \(I_1=\{\lfloor n/2\rfloor+1,\ldots,n\}\), \(m_j=|I_j|\), and \(L=\log(en)\). Set \(A=6\), and put \(\lambda_0=256\), \(b_0=4096\), \(D_A=8\log(9A/8)=8\log(27/4)\), and
\[
\alpha_0=\min\left\{1,\frac1{32\log A},\frac1{256D_A}\right\}.
\]
For \(n\ge2\), put \(M(n)=\max\{2,\lfloor\alpha_0L\rfloor\}\) and \(B(n)=b_0L/m_1\). Let \(N_0\) be the least numerical cutoff such that, for every \(n\ge N_0\), \(\alpha_0L\ge2\), \(4M(n)^2\le m_1\), and \(4M(n)/m_1\le3B(n)/4\). For \(n\ge N_0\), abbreviate \(M=M(n)\), \(B=B(n)\), and set
\[
\widehat{\mathcal H}_n=\left\{k:\sum_{a,y}N^{(0)}_{aky}>\lambda_0L\right\},\qquad \widehat{\mathcal L}_n=\widehat{\mathcal H}_n^c.
\]
Put \(N_k^{(1)}=\sum_{a,y}N^{(1)}_{aky}\), \(N_{ak}^{(1)}=\sum_yN^{(1)}_{aky}\), and
\[
\widehat h_k=\frac{N_k^{(1)}}{m_1}\left\{\frac{N^{(1)}_{1k1}}{N^{(1)}_{1k}}\mathbf1\{N^{(1)}_{1k}>0\}-\frac{N^{(1)}_{0k1}}{N^{(1)}_{0k}}\mathbf1\{N^{(1)}_{0k}>0\}\right\}.
\]
Define \(H_M(x)=\{1-T_M(1-2x)\}/(2M^2)\), \(E_M(x)=H_M(x)/x\), and \(G_M(x)=\{1-E_M(x)\}/x\), using polynomial continuation at zero, and
\[
P_{M,B}(u)=\frac{s(u)t_1(u)}B G_M\!\left(\frac{s_1(u)}B\right)-\frac{s(u)t_0(u)}B G_M\!\left(\frac{s_0(u)}B\right).
\]
Here \(s_a(u)=u_{a0}+u_{a1}\), \(s(u)=s_0(u)+s_1(u)\), \(t_1(u)=u_{11}\), and \(t_0(u)=u_{01}\). Expand \(P_{M,B}(u)=\sum_{|r|\le M}c_ru^r\), set
\[
\widehat P_k=\sum_{|r|\le M}c_r\frac{\prod_{a,y}(N^{(1)}_{aky})_{r_{ay}}}{(m_1)_{|r|}},\quad \widehat T_{\mathcal H}=\sum_{k\in\widehat{\mathcal H}_n}\widehat h_k,\quad \widehat T_{\mathcal L}=\sum_{k\in\widehat{\mathcal L}_n}\widehat P_k,
\]
and define \(\widehat\tau_n^{\mathrm{hyb}}=\max\{-1,\min(1,\widehat T_{\mathcal H}+\widehat T_{\mathcal L})\}\). For \(n<N_0\), set \(\widehat{\mathcal L}_n=\varnothing\) and use the ratio branch on every category. The estimator uses no overlap constant and is computable in \(O(dM^4)\) arithmetic operations.

items.label: Definition P-6
items.nl: \(\mathsf R_{n,d,\epsilon}:=\inf_{\widehat\tau}\sup_{P^{\otimes n}\in\mathcal E_{n,d,\epsilon}}E_P[(\widehat\tau-\tau(P))^2]\), where the infimum is over measurable estimators based on \(O_1,\ldots,O_n\).

items.label: Definition P-7
items.nl: For \(d=2\), set \(p_1=p_2=1/2\), \(\pi_1=\epsilon\), \(\pi_2=1-\epsilon\), \(\mu_{01}=\mu_{02}=0\), \(\mu_{11}=1\), and \(\mu_{12}=0\). Define a full-data extension by drawing \((Y(0),Y(1))=(0,\mathbf 1\{X=1\})\) and then drawing \(A\mid X=k\sim\mathsf{Bernoulli}(\pi_k)\).

items.label: Lemma L-1
items.nl: There are constants \(C_\epsilon,c_\epsilon>0\) and a computable calibration of \(def:hybrid-estimator-handle\) such that, for every \(d\le c_\epsilon n\log n\) and every \(P^{\otimes n}\in\mathcal E_{n,d,\epsilon}\), its light-cell contribution \(\widehat T_{\mathcal L}\) obeys \[ E_P\!\left[\left\{\widehat T_{\mathcal L}-\sum_{k\in\widehat{\mathcal L}_n}\phi(q_k)\right\}^2\right]\le C_\epsilon\left\{\frac{1}{n}+\frac{d^2}{n^2\log^2 n}\right\}. \] The calibration uses a polynomial approximation of \(\phi\) on the overlap-restricted four-cell cone and factorial-moment monomials from \(I_1\).

items.label: Lemma L-2
items.nl:
Let \(m_0\asymp n\), \(L=\log(en)\), \(\widehat{\mathcal H}=\{k:N_k^{(0)}>tL\}\), \(\widehat{\mathcal L}=\widehat{\mathcal H}^c\), \(B_-=tL/(2m_0)\), and \(B_+=2tL/m_0\). For every \(K>0\), sufficiently large numerical \(t\) gives, uniformly over \(d\le cnL\),
\[
P\left(\widehat{\mathcal H}\not\subseteq\{k:p_k\ge B_-\}\text{ or }\widehat{\mathcal L}\not\subseteq\{k:p_k\le B_+\}\right)\le C_Kn^{-K}.
\]
The choice \(t=256\) is valid for \(K=4\).

items.label: Lemma L-3
items.nl:
For the threshold \(\lambda_0=256\), every fixed \(0<\epsilon<1/2\) admits \(C_\epsilon,\rho_\epsilon>0\) and \(N_\epsilon<\infty\) such that, for \(n\ge N_\epsilon\), \(d\le\rho_\epsilon n\log n\), and \(P^{\otimes n}\in\mathcal E_{n,d,\epsilon}\),
\[
E_P\left[\left\{\widehat T_{\mathcal H}-\sum_{k\in\widehat{\mathcal H}_n}\phi(q_k)\right\}^2\right]\le C_\epsilon\left\{\frac1n+\frac{d^2}{n^2\log^2n}\right\}.
\]

items.label: Lemma L-4
items.nl:
For fixed \(0<\epsilon<1/2\), there are \(a_\epsilon,b_\epsilon>0\) such that, for all sufficiently large \(n\) and \(d\le b_\epsilon n\log n\),
\[
\mathsf R_{n,d,\epsilon}\ge a_\epsilon\left\{\frac1n+\frac{d^2}{n^2\log^2n}\right\}.
\]

items.label: Lemma L-5
items.nl:
Define the centered estimator
\[
\widehat\tau_{\mathrm{ctr}}=\frac2n\sum_{i=1}^n(2A_i-1)\left(Y_i-\frac12\right).
\]
For every \(n,d\in\mathbb N\), every \(0<\epsilon\le1/2\), and every \(P^{\otimes n}\in\mathcal E_{n,d,\epsilon}\),
\[
E_P[(\widehat\tau_{\mathrm{ctr}}-\tau(P))^2]\le\frac1n+4(1/2-\epsilon)^2.
\]

items.label: Lemma L-8
items.nl:
Fix \(0<\epsilon<1/2\) and constants \(C_\epsilon,\rho_\epsilon,N_\epsilon\) for which the upper bound in Theorem~\ref{thm:sharp-minimax-fixed-interior} holds. For \(n\ge N_\epsilon\) and \(d\le\rho_\epsilon n\log n\), choose the hybrid when
\[
C_\epsilon\left\{\frac1n+\frac{d^2}{n^2\log^2n}\right\}\le\frac1n+4(1/2-\epsilon)^2,
\]
and otherwise choose \(\widehat\tau_{\mathrm{ctr}}\). The selected estimator has worst-case risk at most
\[
K_\epsilon\left[\frac1n+\min\left\{\frac{d^2}{n^2\log^2n},(1/2-\epsilon)^2\right\}\right],\qquad K_\epsilon=\max\{C_\epsilon,4\}.
\]

items.label: Theorem T-1
items.nl: The full-data law in \(def:two-category-witness\) satisfies \(ass:consistency\), \(ass:conditional-exchangeability\), and \(ass:overlap\), has \(\tau(P)=1/2\), and has naive observed contrast \(E_P[Y\mid A=1]-E_P[Y\mid A=0]=\epsilon\).

items.label: Theorem T-2
items.nl:
For every fixed \(0<\epsilon<1/2\), there are constants \(a_\epsilon,\rho_\epsilon,C_\epsilon>0\) and \(N_\epsilon<\infty\) such that, for every \(n\ge N_\epsilon\) and uniformly over \(d\le\rho_\epsilon n\log n\), the balanced ratio-polynomial hybrid in \(def:hybrid-estimator-handle\) satisfies
\[
a_\epsilon\left\{\frac1n+\frac{d^2}{n^2\log^2n}\right\}\le\mathsf R_{n,d,\epsilon}\le\sup_{P^{\otimes n}\in\mathcal E_{n,d,\epsilon}}E_P[(\widehat\tau_n^{\mathrm{hyb}}-\tau(P))^2]\le C_\epsilon\left\{\frac1n+\frac{d^2}{n^2\log^2n}\right\}.
\]
Consequently, the minimax MSE has parametric order \(n^{-1}\) whenever \(d=O(\sqrt n\log n)\), and, along sequences in the displayed range, it converges to zero if and only if \(d=o(n\log n)\).

items.label: Theorem T-3
items.nl:
The following conclusions hold.

\(\mathrm{(i)}\) The single computable hybrid \(\widehat\tau_n^{\mathrm{hyb}}\), with \(A=6\), \(\lambda_0=256\), \(b_0=4096\), and numerical \(\alpha_0\), does not take \(\epsilon\) as input. For every fixed \(0<\epsilon<1/2\), there are \(C_\epsilon,\rho_\epsilon>0\) and \(N_\epsilon<\infty\) such that, for every \(n\ge N_\epsilon\) and \(d\le\rho_\epsilon n\log n\),
\[
\sup_{P^{\otimes n}\in\mathcal E_{n,d,\epsilon}}E_P[(\widehat\tau_n^{\mathrm{hyb}}-\tau(P))^2]\le C_\epsilon\left\{\frac1n+\frac{d^2}{n^2\log^2n}\right\}.
\]
Thus fixed-class overlap adaptation requires no selector.

\(\mathrm{(ii)}\) For every \(n,d\in\mathbb N\) and \(0<\epsilon\le1/2\), the centered estimator
\[
\widehat\tau_{\mathrm{ctr}}=\frac2n\sum_{i=1}^n(2A_i-1)\left(Y_i-\frac12\right)
\]
satisfies
\[
\sup_{P^{\otimes n}\in\mathcal E_{n,d,\epsilon}}E_P[(\widehat\tau_{\mathrm{ctr}}-\tau(P))^2]\le\frac1n+4(1/2-\epsilon)^2.
\]

\(\mathrm{(iii)}\) For every \(n,d\in\mathbb N\),
\[
\frac{1}{100n}\le\mathsf R_{n,d,1/2}\le\frac1n.
\]

\(\mathrm{(iv)}\) Fix \(0<\epsilon<1/2\) and constants \(C_\epsilon,\rho_\epsilon,N_\epsilon\) from the fixed-interior upper theorem. For \(n\ge N_\epsilon\) and \(d\le\rho_\epsilon n\log n\), choose the hybrid when
\[
C_\epsilon\left\{\frac1n+\frac{d^2}{n^2\log^2n}\right\}\le\frac1n+4(1/2-\epsilon)^2,
\]
and otherwise choose \(\widehat\tau_{\mathrm{ctr}}\). This deterministic choice satisfies
\[
\mathsf R_{n,d,\epsilon}\le K_\epsilon\left[\frac1n+\min\left\{\frac{d^2}{n^2\log^2n},(1/2-\epsilon)^2\right\}\right],\qquad K_\epsilon=\max\{C_\epsilon,4\}.
\]

\(\mathrm{(v)}\) These fixed-class and endpoint conclusions do not assert a matching lower envelope for triangular arrays \(\epsilon=\epsilon_n\).

items.label: Cited result lem:zeng-one-arm-minimax-lower
items.nl:
Fix \(0<\epsilon<1/2\). Let \(\mathcal D_0^{(n,d)}(\epsilon)\) be the fixed-sample binary-outcome subclass of \(\mathcal E_{n,d,\epsilon}\) consisting of laws \(P^{\otimes n}\) with arbitrary masses \(p_k\ge0\), \(\sum_{k=1}^d p_k=1\), propensities satisfying \(\epsilon\le\pi_k\le1-\epsilon\) on every positive-mass category, arbitrary treated response means \(\mu_{1k}\in[0,1]\), and \(\mu_{0k}=0\) for every \(k\). Define \(\psi_1(P)=\sum_{k=1}^d p_k\mu_{1k}\). There are constants \(a_\epsilon,b_\epsilon>0\) and \(N_\epsilon<\infty\) such that, for all \(n\ge N_\epsilon\) and \(d\le b_\epsilon n\log n\),
\[
\inf_{\widehat\psi}\sup_{P^{\otimes n}\in\mathcal D_0^{(n,d)}(\epsilon)}E_P[(\widehat\psi-\psi_1(P))^2]\ge a_\epsilon\left\{\frac1n+\frac{d^2}{n^2\log^2n}\right\},
\]
where the infimum is over measurable estimators based on the fixed sample of size \(n\).
